cards.front: **Ping fails between hosts in different VLANs across switches.** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
ping fails — VLAN A host → VLAN B host (across switches)
   │
   ▼
show interfaces fa0/1 switchport on BOTH switches
   │
   ▼
Operational Mode = trunk on both?
   │
no ─► fix access-stuck side: switchport mode trunk
   │
yes
   ▼
show interfaces trunk
   │
   ▼
Native VLAN matches? Allowed VLAN list includes both VLANs?
   │
no ─► fix: switchport trunk native vlan X / allowed vlan add Y
   │
yes
   ▼
L3 gateway exists for both VLANs? (SVI or ROAS sub-interface)
   │
no ─► configure SVI: interface vlan X / ip address / no shut
   │
yes
   ▼
ip routing enabled on the L3 switch?
   │
no ─► conf t / ip routing
```

cards.front: **OSPF neighbor stuck or won't form.** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
OSPF neighbor down / never reaches FULL
   │
   ▼
show ip ospf neighbor — what state is it in?
   │
Down  ─► no Hello seen. Check: interface up? OSPF process running?
        network statement matches? ACL blocking proto 89?
   │
Init  ─► one-way. Check matching Hello/Dead timers, area ID,
        authentication, subnet on link
   │
2-Way (between DROthers) ─► EXPECTED on broadcast. Not a bug.
   │
ExStart/Exchange stuck ─► MTU MISMATCH. Fix:
        ip ospf mtu-ignore  (or set matching MTU both ends)
   │
   ▼
show ip ospf interface gX/Y — area + timers + auth must match neighbor
```

cards.front: **EtherChannel won't bundle (Po stays down).** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
EtherChannel members exist but Po flag = I (stand-alone) or D (down)
   │
   ▼
show etherchannel summary — what flags on member ports?
   │
I (stand-alone) ─► LACP/PAgP negotiation failed. Check:
                   • Both sides active+active OR active+passive (not passive+passive)
                   • Same channel-protocol on both sides
                   • channel-group X mode <correct mode>
   │
s (suspended)   ─► Config mismatch between member ports. Check:
                   • speed, duplex, switchport mode, native VLAN,
                     allowed VLAN list, STP settings — ALL must match
   │
D (down)        ─► All member interfaces are down. show ip int brief.
   │
   ▼
show lacp neighbor — does both sides report each other?
```

```
ssh -l admin <ip>  → fails
   │
   ▼
Ping reaches device?
   │
no ─► IP/route/ACL problem. show ip route, show access-list
   │
yes
   ▼
show ip ssh — version 2? RSA key generated?
   │
no key ─► crypto key generate rsa modulus 2048
   │
   ▼
show running-config | section line vty
   │
   ▼
transport input ssh? login local? username defined?
   │
missing ─► add: transport input ssh / login local /
                username admin secret <pw>
   │
   ▼
ip domain-name set? (required for RSA key gen)
   │
no ─► ip domain-name lab.local then re-gen RSA
   │
   ▼
Check ACL on VTY: access-class? — blocking source?
```

cards.front: **DHCP client gets 169.254.x.x (APIPA — no IP).** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
Client shows APIPA (169.254.x.x)  — never got DHCP lease
   │
   ▼
Is client port in correct VLAN? show int gX/Y switchport
   │
no ─► fix: switchport access vlan X
   │
yes
   ▼
DHCP server reachable from client's VLAN gateway?
   │
   ▼
On gateway router/L3 switch: ip helper-address configured on SVI?
(needed if DHCP server is in different subnet)
   │
no ─► interface vlan X / ip helper-address <dhcp-server-ip>
   │
yes
   ▼
show ip dhcp pool — pool exists for this network? IPs available?
   │
exhausted ─► extend pool range or reduce lease time
   │
   ▼
show ip dhcp conflict — IP conflict detected?
   │
yes ─► clear ip dhcp conflict *
   │
   ▼
debug ip dhcp server events / packet — watch for DHCP DISCOVER arriving
```

cards.front: **NAT translations not happening (LAN host can't reach internet).** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
LAN host ping 8.8.8.8 fails
   │
   ▼
ping 8.8.8.8 from the NAT router itself — works?
   │
no ─► routing issue, not NAT. Check default route + ISP link.
   │
yes
   ▼
show ip nat translations — any entries when LAN host pings?
   │
empty
   ▼
Outside interface marked? show run | include nat outside
   │
no ─► interface <wan> / ip nat outside
   │
   ▼
Inside interface(s) marked? ip nat inside
   │
no ─► interface <lan> / ip nat inside
   │
   ▼
ip nat inside source list/route-map + overload — configured?
ACL referenced by NAT rule actually matches LAN traffic?
   │
   ▼
show ip nat statistics — Hits should increment as LAN host pings
```

cards.front: **Static route doesn't appear in `show ip route`.** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
ip route added in running-config but no S in show ip route
   │
   ▼
Exit interface or next-hop reachable?
   │
   ▼
For next-hop static (ip route X.X.X.0 mask via Y.Y.Y.Y):
   • show ip route Y.Y.Y.Y — recursive lookup must resolve
   • If next-hop not in RIB → route invalid, won't install
   │
For interface static (ip route X.X.X.0 mask gX/Y):
   • show ip int brief gX/Y — must be up/up
   • If admin-down → no shutdown
   │
   ▼
Another route with lower AD for same prefix?
   • show ip route X.X.X.0 — what source?
   • Floating static intentionally higher AD = expected hiding
   │
   ▼
Track configured + tracked object Down?
   • show track — if down, static is suppressed by design
```

cards.front: **Inter-VLAN routing on ROAS broken (PCs in same VLAN work, different VLANs don't).** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
PCs in VLAN X can talk to each other but not to VLAN Y
   │
   ▼
On router — sub-interfaces exist for both VLANs?
   show ip int brief — gX/Y.10, gX/Y.20, etc?
   │
no ─► create sub-interface: encapsulation dot1q <vlan> / ip address
   │
yes
   ▼
encapsulation dot1q <vlan> matches actual VLAN ID?
   │
no ─► fix encapsulation
   │
yes
   ▼
On switch — trunk to router carries both VLANs?
   show interfaces trunk
   │
   ▼
Allowed VLAN list includes both? Native VLAN matches router's "native" sub-interface?
   │
   ▼
Client default gateway = router sub-interface IP for its VLAN?
   ipconfig / ifconfig — gateway correct?
```

```
Interface in err-disabled state — link unusable
   │
   ▼
show interfaces gX/Y status — "err-disabled"
show interfaces gX/Y — "Last input: violation"
   │
   ▼
show port-security interface gX/Y
   • Max MAC: how many allowed?
   • Total MAC count: how many seen?
   • Last source: which MAC triggered?
   • Violation count: should be > 0
   │
   ▼
Cause:
   • Wrong device plugged in
   • PC + phone behind port (max=1 too restrictive — needs max=2)
   • Sticky MACs locked + device replaced
   │
   ▼
Recovery (manual):
   shutdown
   no shutdown

cards.front: **ACL blocking unexpected traffic (legitimate flow denied).** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
Traffic that "should work" is being denied
   │
   ▼
show access-lists — read top-down, first match wins
   │
   ▼
For each line, check Hits/Matches counter — incrementing = catching this traffic
   │
   ▼
show ip interface gX/Y | include access list
   • Which ACL is applied? Inbound or outbound?
   │
   ▼
Wildcard mask correct? (common typo: subnet mask instead of wildcard)
   • 0.0.0.0 = exact match one host
   • 0.0.0.255 = match /24 network
   • 255.255.255.255 = match anything
   │
   ▼
Implicit deny at end — is the legitimate flow explicitly permitted?
   • show ip access-list — last line should be permit if you want anything else allowed
   │
   ▼
Placement rule:
   • Standard ACL → close to DESTINATION (only matches src IP)
   • Extended ACL → close to SOURCE
```

cards.front: **HSRP not failing over to Standby when Active should be down.** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
Active router goes down — clients lose connectivity (no failover)
   │
   ▼
show standby brief on Standby router — what state?
   │
Listen/Speak ─► hasn't been elected Standby yet. Wait for hold timer (default 10s).
   │
Standby      ─► should be promoting now. Check hold timer expiring properly.
   │
   ▼
Hold timer too long? Tune for faster failover:
   standby 1 timers 1 3      ! hello 1s, hold 3s (both sides match)
   │
   ▼
If "Active" router is just reloading + coming back up faster than Standby promotes:
   • Preempt OFF by default — Active won't take over from "lower priority"
   • Issue: did Active actually go down, or just flap?
   │
   ▼
Tracking working? show track — if track Up but Active priority decremented
   correctly, Standby should win priority race
   │
   ▼
Both sides see each other?
   • show standby — same vIP, same group #, same auth on both
```

cards.front: **Time wrong on device (NTP not syncing).** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
show clock shows wrong time
   │
   ▼
show ntp status
   • Clock is unsynchronized? → not pulling from server
   • Stratum 16 = unsynchronized
   │
   ▼
show ntp associations
   • Server listed? Stratum reasonable? "*" = sync peer
   • No "*" = found server but can't sync
   │
   ▼
Ping NTP server — reachable?
   │
no ─► routing problem or ACL blocking UDP 123
   │
yes
   ▼
ACL allows UDP 123 inbound (responses)?
   ! NTP uses UDP 123 source AND destination
   │
   ▼
Authentication mismatch?
   show ntp authentication-keys
   If keys configured but wrong → won't sync
   │
   ▼
Timezone correct? clock timezone CST -6 (then NTP sync sets UTC underneath)
```

cards.front: **Interface "up/down" — physical up but line protocol down.** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
show ip interface brief shows: g0/1 up down
   │
   ▼
"up" = layer 1 (physical signal OK)
"down" = layer 2 (no protocol handshake)
   │
   ▼
For Ethernet — usually a keepalive issue, less common
For Serial — check encapsulation matches both sides (PPP, HDLC)
   • show interface s0/0 — "encapsulation HDLC" or "encapsulation PPP"
   │
   ▼
If serial PPP — authentication mismatch (PAP/CHAP credentials wrong)
For Ethernet — duplex/speed mismatch on rare occasions
   │
   ▼
shutdown / no shutdown — sometimes resets the line protocol negotiation
   │
   ▼
For DTE/DCE serial — DCE side must have clock rate set
   interface s0/0 / clock rate 64000
```

cards.front: **Cannot reach hosts on a directly-connected subnet (ARP fails).** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
Router has interface in subnet but can't ping hosts
   │
   ▼
show ip arp — destination IP resolved?
   │
no ARP entry ─► host not responding to ARP, or ARP request not reaching
   • Check L2: STP blocking? VLAN mismatch? Trunk allow this VLAN?
   • Check L1: cable, link light, duplex
   │
ARP entry says "Incomplete" ─► request sent, no reply
   • Host firewall blocking ARP?
   • Host actually on this subnet? (IP/mask mismatch — host thinks gateway is elsewhere)
   │
   ▼
debug arp — see ARP requests + replies in real time
(use briefly — chatty on busy interfaces)
   │
   ▼
clear ip arp <ip> — force fresh ARP resolution
```

cards.front: **Console doesn't prompt for password / locks you out.** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
Plug into console — get prompt immediately, no password
   │
   ▼
show running-config | section line con
   │
   ▼
No "password X" or "login" command? → no password protection (DEFAULT)
   • This is the factory default — IOS does NOT enforce console password
   │
   ▼
Fix: 
   line con 0
    password ConsolePass1!
    login                                ! enables password check
    exec-timeout 10 0
   │
   ▼
Same approach for auxiliary line:
   line aux 0
    password ...
    login
   │
   ▼
LOCKED OUT (forgot password)?
   • Reload while pressing Ctrl+Break → ROMmon
   • confreg 0x2142  (skip startup-config on next boot)
   • reset → boots without config → set new password → save
```

cards.front: **Trunk drops some VLANs but not others.** Diagnostic flow?
cards.back:
```
VLAN 10 works across trunk, VLAN 20 doesn't
   │
   ▼
show interfaces trunk on BOTH sides
   • "Vlans allowed on trunk" — does it include both 10 and 20?
   • "Vlans allowed and active in management domain" — does it list both?
   • "Vlans in spanning tree forwarding state" — is VLAN 20 forwarding or blocking?
   │
   ▼
Allowed list missing 20?
   switchport trunk allowed vlan add 20
   (DO use "add" — without it, you REPLACE the list)
   │
   ▼
VLAN 20 not created on this switch?
   vlan 20
    name Servers
   │
   ▼
STP blocking on this trunk for VLAN 20?
   show spanning-tree vlan 20
   Look for "BLK" (blocking) on trunk port
   • Different STP topology than VLAN 10 — root may be elsewhere
   │
   ▼
DTP issue — operational mode = access for this VLAN?
   show interfaces switchport — verify trunk operational on both sides
```

```
Slow throughput, errors, partial connectivity
   │
   ▼
show interfaces gX/Y
   • Look for: "input errors" + "CRC" + "frame" + "late collisions"
   • Late collisions on full-duplex = duplex mismatch (impossible on true full-duplex)
   │
   ▼
show interfaces gX/Y | include duplex
   "Half-duplex" vs "Full-duplex"
   │
   ▼
Compare BOTH ends of the link — must be consistent:
   • Both auto → fine
   • Both hardcoded same → fine
   • One auto, one hardcoded → MISMATCH (auto side falls back to half-duplex)
   │
   ▼
Fix:
   interface gX/Y
    duplex full
    speed 1000
   (or `duplex auto / speed auto` on BOTH sides)
   │
   ▼
After change: clear counters gX/Y, monitor for fresh errors
```
